steps.title: Close the interruption
steps.content: The phrase “because of its malleability” interrupts the relative clause about slate. The blank must close that interrupting phrase and supply the modal verb needed before “be easily carved.”

steps.title: Reject faulty punctuation
steps.content: A uses a dash where the interrupting phrase needs a comma. C provides no punctuation to close the interruption. D leaves the interruption unclosed and separates “could” from “be easily carved.” The comma plus “could” completes the grammar. The answer is B.
